summary: Added a new C translation unit for the common fighter bury action and removed most of the corresponding assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s. The PR only partially matched the file: the report shows src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Bury.c at 95.29%, with many functions fully matching and several larger functions kept behind ASM/MUST_MATCH fallbacks. The work also typed bury-related fighter/common-data fields, added ftCommon_BuryType and lb_UnkAnimStruct, fixed helper prototypes needed by the translation, and adjusted many ground/stage callback signatures and header placements to support bury logic that queries ground animation data.

key_files.role: New partial C implementation of ftCo_Bury; report shows 95.29% file match with several 100% functions and larger partial functions.
New header with typed declarations for ftCo_Bury functions split out of ft_0C08.h.
Removed most assembly for the 0C0874-0C124C bury range while keeping later assembly and literals.
Added ftCo_Bury.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES before the remaining ft_0C08.s object.
Named bury fields in Fighter and ftCommonData, added gr/types include, and refined related field types.
Added mv.co.bury motion-vars layout with timer/translation/collision-box fields.
Added ftCommon_BuryType enum and adjusted FIGHTERVARS_SIZE from 0x100 to 0xF8.
Retyped ftColl_80076640, ftColl_80076764, and ftColl_80078384 signatures used by bury collision setup.
Changed stage callback5 to return lb_UnkAnimStruct* instead of bool.
Renamed/introduced struct lb_UnkAnimStruct with x0_data and x4_size fields.
New header declaring pl_8003EC30, used by bury collision/bonus reporting.

what_changed: Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Bury.c with implementations/decomp attempts for ftCo_800C0874 through ftCo_800C124C plus ftCo_Bury_Anim, ftCo_Bury_IASA, ftCo_Bury_Phys, and ftCo_Bury_Coll.
Removed hundreds of assembly lines for the newly translated bury range from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s and added ftCo_Bury.c.o to obj_files.mk.
Kept partial or non-matching functions as ASM functions with C bodies under `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` and assembly fallbacks under the opposite path.
Added ftCommon_BuryType with BuryType_Unk0 through BuryType_Unk3 and used it in ftCo_Bury function signatures.
Named Fighter fields at fp+2324/fp+2328/fp+232C as bury_stage_kind, bury_timer_1, and bury_timer_2.
Expanded ftCommonData around +5DC through +620 to expose bury_timer_unk1, bury_timer_unk2, bury_timer_unk3, x5F4, and several float tuning fields used by bury duration/damage calculations.
Added a `bury` member to `union ftCommon_MotionVars` containing x0, ftCollisionBox coll_box, x1C, x20, and Vec3 translate.
Retyped collision helpers so bury code can pass `Fighter*`, `float*`, `HurtCapsule*`, and `HitCapsule*` instead of generic unknown parameters.
Retyped lb collision helper lbColl_80008D30 second argument from int to UNK_T and introduced lb_UnkAnimStruct in lb/types.h and lb/forward.h.
Retyped ground/stage callback helpers such as Ground_801C5700 and multiple gr* callback5 functions from bool/int-style stubs to `lb_UnkAnimStruct* (...)`, usually returning NULL in stage stubs.
Moved many stage-local static prototypes out of headers and into their corresponding .c files, reducing header pollution while preserving static linkage.
Added plbonuslib.h for pl_8003EC30 and used it for bury-related player bonus/reporting calls.
smart_moves: Separated the newly translated bury range into ftCo_Bury.c/ftCo_Bury.h while leaving later ft_0C08 functions in ft_0C08.h/asm, making the partial decomp boundary explicit.
Used typed field names for bury state only where the PR had direct evidence from the translated code and offsets: bury_stage_kind, bury_timer_1, bury_timer_2, and bury_timer_unk1/2/3.
Kept fragile functions build-safe with ASM wrappers and C-under-WIP structure instead of forcing incomplete matches into the default MUST_MATCH path.
Converted stage callback5/Ground_801C5700 to return lb_UnkAnimStruct*, matching the bury code's need to read `x4_size` and pass animation data into collision helpers.
Cleaned up static stage prototypes by placing them in .c files rather than public headers, which helped resolve type changes without exporting internal functions.
Preserved literal symbols and force-active handling for ftCo_Bury constants and assert strings, which is important for matching rodata/sdata placement.
Adjusted fighter variable padding using FIGHTERVARS_SIZE rather than hard-coded 0x100 in character-specific fighter vars affected by the layout change.
decomp_lessons: When a new action relies on many cross-system helpers, matching the action often requires typing adjacent APIs first; here bury needed ftcoll, lbcollision, Ground_801C5700, stage callbacks, plbonuslib, and fighter common data.

Stage callback return types can be inferred from a consumer: ftCo_Bury expects a pointer with an `x4_size` field, so callback5 and Ground_801C5700 were better modeled as returning `lb_UnkAnimStruct*` than bool.
A partially matched file can still remove a large assembly island if unmatched functions keep inline assembly fallbacks under MUST_MATCH and C code is available under WIP/non-MUST_MATCH.
Naming fields at known offsets enables downstream matches: fp+2324/fp+2328/fp+232C became bury_stage_kind/bury_timer_1/bury_timer_2 and then replaced generic uses such as dst->x232C copies.
Keep public headers narrow: internal stage static declarations belong in the .c file, especially when signatures are still evolving for decomp.
For motion-state-specific storage, adding a named `mv.co.bury` struct is preferable to spreading raw offsets across the action implementation.
Use offset comments in struct definitions when adding fields around fragile packed layouts; this PR kept comments such as `/* +5DC */` and `/* fp+2324 */` to verify alignment.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action files use `ftCo_<Action>.c` and `ftCo_<Action>.h`; this PR added `ftCo_Bury.c` and `ftCo_Bury.h`.
Action callbacks use the standard suffixes `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll` for `ftCo_Bury_*` functions.
Unidentified bury types were named with an enum rather than raw integers: `ftCommon_BuryType` and `BuryType_Unk0` through `BuryType_Unk3`.
Bury-related Fighter fields were named semantically but conservatively: `bury_stage_kind`, `bury_timer_1`, and `bury_timer_2`.
Unidentified common-data bury timers were named `bury_timer_unk1`, `bury_timer_unk2`, and `bury_timer_unk3`, preserving uncertainty while documenting purpose.
Library animation data was normalized as `lb_UnkAnimStruct` with fields `x0_data` and `x4_size` instead of the prior `_UnkAnimStruct`/`UnkAnimStruct` naming.
Header guards for address-range split headers used GALE01-style guards, e.g. `GALE01_0C12D8` for the remaining ft_0C08.h range.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `ASM void` functions with C implementations under `#if !defined(MUST_MATCH) || defined(WIP)` and assembly bodies in the else branch for partial matches.
Used `#pragma peephole on` around assembly-fallback sections to preserve CodeWarrior behavior.
Used `#ifdef WIP #pragma force_active on` to keep literals/assert strings active when building WIP C paths.
Kept explicit extern literal declarations such as ftCo_804D8C28, ftCo_804D8C30, ftCo_804D8C38, ftCo_804D8C3C and assert string symbols for rodata/sdata stability.
Used a MUST_MATCH-only unused stack array in ftCo_800C0D0C (`u8 _[8] = { 0 };`) to address stack layout differences, according to the inline todo.
Matched small switch functions in C directly, such as ftCo_800C0874, ftCo_800C09B4, ftCo_800C0A28, ftCo_800C0A98, ftCo_800C0C88, and ftCo_800C0CB8.
Left larger functions such as ftCo_800C08A0, ftCo_800C0B20, ftCo_800C0D0C, ftCo_800C0FCC, and ftCo_Bury_Phys partially matched with assembly fallbacks as indicated by the PR report.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence is the match report for ftCo_Bury.c; no explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes are available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_Bury_Phys, ftCo_800C0D0C, ftCo_800C0B20, ftCo_800C08A0, or ftCo_800C0FCC.
Search for follow-up renames of ftCommon_BuryType and BuryType_Unk* to determine actual bury source/type meanings.
Search for later expansion of lb_UnkAnimStruct fields beyond x0_data and x4_size.
Search for later Ground_801C5700 and StageCallbacks callback5 typing improvements.
Search for later cleanup of ftCo_804D8C28/ftCo_804D8C30/ftCo_804D8C38/ftCo_804D8C3C literal externs.
Search for future PRs that rename p_ftCommonData fields x5F4 through x614 used in bury duration and damage formulas.
